Executive Board.persona_brief: Non-technical. Cares about risk, cost, strategic alignment, and AI governance.
talking_points: We have real-time visibility into the health of every application in the portfolio.
AI agents are governed — every one is tracked, classified, and reviewed on schedule.
Capability gaps are detected automatically — no manual surveys required.

questions.q: What percentage of our application portfolio is at risk?
questions.why_it_lands: Converts abstract graph data into a board-level risk metric.
questions.fallback: Navigate to Portfolio tab — show the TIME quadrant chart directly.

questions.q: Which business capabilities have no technology support?
questions.why_it_lands: Reveals blind spots — capabilities the business relies on with no app enablement.
questions.fallback: Ask: 'List capabilities with no supporting application' (simpler SPARQL).

questions.q: What is our AI governance posture across all agents?
questions.why_it_lands: AI governance is a board-level concern post EU AI Act.
questions.fallback: Navigate to AI Governance tab — shows unrated and overdue agents in a table.

CTO.persona_brief: Technical. Cares about technical debt, platform risk, dependency chains, lifecycle.
talking_points: Every application has a technical fitness score — scored on lifecycle, platform modernity, and integration.
Risky dependencies are flagged automatically — sunset apps with active downstreams.
Background agents run nightly health scans so technical debt is tracked continuously.

questions.q: Which applications are on sunset or legacy lifecycle?
questions.fallback: Navigate to Portfolio tab — filter by TIME class 'ELIMINATE'.

questions.q: What are the riskiest dependencies in our portfolio?
questions.why_it_lands: Shows the blast radius of technical debt — not just the debt itself.
questions.fallback: Navigate to Change Impact tab — pick any ELIMINATE-class app and run impact analysis.

questions.q: Show me all AI agents without a risk tier assigned.
questions.why_it_lands: Compliance gap — AI Act requires risk classification.
questions.fallback: Navigate to AI Governance tab — unrated agents listed in the first panel.

CDTO.persona_brief: Digital transformation. Cares about capabilities, data, business outcomes, transformation roadmap.
talking_points: Every capability is mapped to the applications that enable it — and the gaps are visible.
Change impact analysis shows which business processes are affected before any decision is made.
The graph links data assets, capabilities, and applications into a single transformation view.

questions.why_it_lands: Shows process-to-technology traceability at the click of a button.
questions.fallback: Ask: 'Which apps enable Sales or Finance capabilities?' (broader)

questions.q: What data assets does the Finance domain rely on?
questions.why_it_lands: Surfaces data dependencies for the CDO/CDTO's data strategy.
questions.fallback: Navigate to Data Query tab — run a domain-scoped data asset query.

questions.q: What is the impact if we retire SAP ERP?
questions.why_it_lands: Real-time change impact — shows every downstream dependency in concentric rings.
questions.fallback: Navigate to Change Impact tab — search for SAP ERP and run analysis.

Chief Architect.persona_brief: Technical depth. Cares about ADRs, integration patterns, architecture standards, SA recommendations.
talking_points: The SA Advisor generates solution architecture recommendations grounded in the live graph.
ADRs are stored in the graph and queryable — decision history is always accessible.
The diagram generator produces ArchiMate-aligned diagrams from natural language.

questions.q: Generate a solution architecture for a new customer portal.
questions.why_it_lands: Live demo of AI-assisted architecture design.
questions.fallback: Navigate to SA Advisor tab — use the pre-built 'Customer Portal' example.

questions.q: List all Architecture Decision Records for the Finance domain.
questions.why_it_lands: Shows institutional memory — ADRs are findable by domain.
questions.fallback: Navigate to SA Health tab — ADR list is in the second panel.

questions.q: What is the blast radius if we decommission the legacy data warehouse?
questions.why_it_lands: Multi-hop impact analysis with concentric dependency rings.
questions.fallback: Navigate to Change Impact tab — search 'data warehouse' and run analysis.
